authoritative_sources.title: Businessperson's Guide to Federal Warranty Law
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public home-services consumer and warranty guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC business guidance is authoritative for federal warranty and service-contract concepts that constrain home-service agreement coverage, disclosure, dispute, and non-legal handling boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: service agreement coverage and service-contract boundary validation

authoritative_sources.title: 16 CFR Part 701 - Disclosure of Written Consumer Product Warranty Terms and Conditions
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public home-services consumer and warranty guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The eCFR is the official public codification of federal regulations. Part 701 defines written warranty disclosure concepts that support covered service, duration, remedy, exclusion, and source-record requirements.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: agreement, warranty, and covered-service evidence validation
used_for: agreement terms version and coverage posture

authoritative_sources.title: 16 CFR Part 429 - Rule Concerning Cooling-off Period for Sales Made at Homes or at Certain Other Locations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public home-services consumer and warranty guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal regulation for covered in-home sales and cancellation notice posture, directly relevant when home-service agreements involve renewal, cancellation, or customer communication boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: agreement exception, cancellation, and customer-communication escalation
used_for: cancellation and in-home sale boundary handling

authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR 424.57 - DMEPOS Supplier Standards
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal supplier standard for Medicare DMEPOS suppliers, including warranty, repair, replacement, delivery proof, safe-use instructions, beneficiary complaint, contact, and documentation duties.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: DMEPOS warranty, repair, maintenance, delivery, and complaint prerequisite handling
used_for: delivery proof and complaint log handling
supplier-standard and Medicare payment boundary escalation

authoritative_sources.title: HIPAA Minimum Necessary Standard
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health & Human Services, Office for Civil Rights
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR is the HIPAA privacy regulator. The guidance constrains PHI use and disclosure in service agreement notes, vendor routing, technician/provider packets, and patient/member records.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: PHI-limited agreement artifact and communication routing

authoritative_sources.title: Uses and Disclosures for Treatment, Payment, and Health Care Operations
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health & Human Services, Office for Civil Rights
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR guidance is authoritative for PHI use and disclosure boundaries in treatment, payment, and healthcare operations contexts that service agreement work may touch.
used_for: payment or healthcare operations PHI boundary

authoritative_sources.title: Interoperability and Prior Authorization
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS prior authorization guidance is authoritative for covered payer authorization workflow expectations and supports escalation when agreement service is blocked by authorization or payer-data status.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: authorization, referral, and eligibility prerequisite handling
used_for: patient access and utilization management routing

authoritative_sources.title: Medicare Benefit Policy Manual, Chapter 7 - Home Health Services
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS manual guidance is authoritative for Medicare home health prerequisites, plan-of-care dependencies, homebound and service qualification concepts, and the need to avoid clinical or medical-necessity determinations in administrative service agreement handling.
used_for: medical necessity and clinical judgment refusal

authoritative_sources.title: United States Core Data for Interoperability Version 6
authoritative_sources.publisher: Assistant Secretary for Technology Policy / Office of the National Coordinator for Health IT
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ASTP/ONC USCDI is the federal healthcare interoperability data baseline. It supports patient, encounter, order, care team, procedure, and clinical note terminology used in EHR, CRM, payer, and service agreement records.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: system-of-record terminology and tenant term normalization

authoritative_sources.title: CAQH CORE Operating Rules
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CAQH CORE operating rules are recognized national healthcare administrative operating rules for eligibility, claim status, prior authorization, payment, and remittance transactions used by providers and health plans.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: eligibility, authorization, claim, payment, and remittance terminology handling

workflow_questions.question: What prerequisites or authorizations gate the workflow?
workflow_questions.answer: Required prerequisites include tenant service model, jurisdiction, business unit, service setting, source hierarchy, RACI, escalation SLA, agreement ID, terms version, contract holder, covered service or asset, effective and expiration dates, cancellation or renewal posture, warranty/service-contract posture, work order, service date, site-readiness and technician/provider notes, privacy constraints, and, when healthcare linked, patient/member status, payer, plan, benefits, eligibility, referral, authorization, order, encounter, DMEPOS delivery or maintenance record, and documentation state. Missing authorization, referral, eligibility, agreement status, covered-service entitlement, DMEPOS proof of delivery, or technician/provider evidence blocks completion.

workflow_questions.question: Which records or systems establish the patient, member, or claim truth set?
workflow_questions.answer: Truth-set systems include service agreement or contract repository, warranty administrator, CRM, scheduling/dispatch, field-service management or work-order platform, technician/provider mobile notes, EHR/EMR, clinical documentation system, practice management, revenue cycle, payer portal, authorization or utilization management system, referral management, eligibility system, case management, document repository, privacy/access-control system, and audit logs. Claim truth remains with revenue cycle, coding, payer operations, or utilization management rather than this lane.

workflow_questions.question: Where does the lane stop and licensed clinical, privacy, or payer-interpretation authority begin?
workflow_questions.answer: The lane stops before diagnosis, treatment planning, clinical need, medical necessity, care-pathway decisions, licensed review, patient-safety signoff, PHI disclosure approval, consent interpretation, payer coverage, authorization approval, referral sufficiency, claim/coding/denial/appeal decisions, legal agreement interpretation, coverage exception approval, price or refund approval, warranty adjudication, recall remedy, licensed trade signoff, dispatch command, technician execution, and safety-critical release.

workflow_stages.name: Service agreement intake and boundary classification
workflow_stages.description: Capture requested action, service model, jurisdiction, agreement record, warranty/service-contract posture, customer or patient/member context, source systems, RACI, escalation SLA, and authority posture before accepting work.
source_classes: Public home-services consumer and warranty guidance

workflow_stages.name: Prerequisite and evidence validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate agreement ID, terms version, contract holder, effective period, covered service, asset or equipment identifier, work order, schedule, technician/provider note, site-readiness evidence, authorization, referral, eligibility, order, encounter, privacy posture, DMEPOS delivery or maintenance evidence, and source-of-truth hierarchy.
source_classes: Public home-services consumer and warranty guidance
Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Lifecycle execution and service agreement progression
workflow_stages.description: Move accepted work through prerequisites_checked, agreement_verified, coverage_or_entitlement_hold, work_order_ready, technician_or_provider_review_needed, in_service, blocked, rework, escalated, completed, or returned_to_orchestrator based on auditable evidence and owner acceptance.
source_classes: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
Public payer or provider operating guidance

workflow_stages.name: Exception queue and rework handling
workflow_stages.description: Handle incomplete artifacts, conflicting systems, agreement or coverage gaps, DMEPOS repair and complaint evidence gaps, schedule or technician/provider blockers, PHI-sensitive communication, payer prerequisites, clinical boundaries, and policy conflicts with explicit owner routing.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Public home-services consumer and warranty guidance

workflow_stages.name: Completion confirmation and evidence capture
workflow_stages.description: Record completion only when agreement disposition, covered service state, work-order or handoff result, prerequisite evidence, source record links, owner acceptance, residual risks, downstream handoff, and reopen trigger are documented.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

artifacts: service agreement, maintenance plan, warranty, service-contract, or product-protection record
agreement ID, terms version, contract holder, covered service or asset, effective period, renewal, cancellation, deductible, trip-charge, and service-frequency posture
CRM case, customer/member contact log, callback note, service request, work order, scheduling record, dispatch record, technician/provider mobile note, and completion criteria
asset, equipment, model, serial, DMEPOS item, proof of delivery, safe-use instruction, maintenance, repair, replacement, complaint, and warranty notice records
eligibility, benefits, referral, authorization, order, encounter, discharge or care-transition, and clinical-administrative notes
charge capture, coding, claim, remittance, denial, appeal, and payer portal references used only as boundary or routing evidence
privacy-controlled routing note, exception queue note, completion evidence record, and audit log
systems_of_record: technician/provider mobile application or field note repository
DMEPOS supplier, delivery, maintenance, complaint, or item repository
EHR or EMR and clinical documentation systems
revenue cycle, charge capture, coding, claim, remittance, denial, or appeal system

allowed: Classify service agreement requests as in scope, out of scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.
Request minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, source links, and owner context needed to continue safely.

Advance, hold, rework, escalate, or complete the lifecycle based on documented agreement, covered-service, work-order, technician/provider, authorization, referral, eligibility, privacy, DMEPOS, and owner evidence.
Prepare service or warranty status summaries, execution status updates, exception queue notes, escalation notes, and completion evidence records.
escalate: Required agreement, warranty, work-order, technician/provider, EHR/EMR, payer, authorization, referral, eligibility, privacy, claim, DMEPOS, or tenant authority records are missing or materially conflict.

The request requires clinical judgment, medical necessity, payer interpretation, PHI disclosure approval, coding, claim, denial, appeal, agreement/legal interpretation, coverage exception approval, price approval, refund approval, warranty adjudication, recall remedy, licensed trade signoff, technician execution, dispatch command, or safety-critical release.
Public guidance, tenant policy, payer rules, state cancellation posture, agreement terms, warranty terms, supplier standards, or system records conflict materially.
A patient-safety, PHI, clinical, site-safety, DMEPOS safe-use, or service-readiness signal changes whether routine agreement progression can continue.
refuse: Mark agreement service complete without documented agreement disposition, covered service status, prerequisite evidence, owner acceptance, and record links.

Approve backdated coverage, discounts, refunds, agreement waivers, cancellation outcomes, payer coverage, authorization approval, medical necessity, coding, claim handling, PHI disclosure, clinical recommendations, recall remedies, or warranty denials.
Authorize field service when technician/provider, licensed trade, safety, infection-control, scope, access, DMEPOS safe-use, or site-readiness blockers require accountable owner review.

domain_failure_modes: service agreement progression proceeds without authorization, referral, eligibility, agreement, covered-service, service-date, or DMEPOS prerequisites
patient/member PHI is included in broad agreement or technician/provider communications without minimum-necessary controls
the lane implies medical necessity, diagnosis, treatment planning, clinical need, or payer coverage while only owning administrative agreement handling
agreement, cancellation, discount, refund, backdating, deductible, trip-charge, or coverage exceptions are approved by the service agreement lane without legal or financial authority
DMEPOS warranty, proof of delivery, safe-use instruction, repair, replacement, maintenance, or complaint evidence gaps are treated as ordinary customer-service friction
technician/provider readiness, site access, infection-control, safety, recall, or licensed trade blockers are treated as routine agreement progression
conflicting CRM, agreement, warranty, dispatch, technician/provider, EHR, payer, or claim records are synthesized into unsupported completion
stale agreement service requests are closed without service disposition, source links, owner acceptance, residual risk, or reopen trigger
tenant terminology is mapped casually and changes the meaning of service plan, entitlement, authorization, referral, agreement hold, or maintenance visit

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Missing authorization, referral, or eligibility dependency
PHI or privacy-sensitive request that requires controlled handling
Boundary case that would require licensed clinical or payer-interpretation escalation
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion
Normal in-scope execution from accepted work to completion
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves public baseline

unresolved_ambiguities.ambiguity: State-specific service contract, home solicitation, contractor, warranty, and cancellation rules vary and may impose requirements beyond federal FTC/eCFR baselines.
unresolved_ambiguities.market_ready_resolution: Not blocking because the pack requires jurisdiction and tenant legal/compliance routing before deciding agreement rights, cancellation outcomes, refunds, or state-law remedies.

unresolved_ambiguities.ambiguity: Tenant agreements, payer contracts, DMEPOS supplier policies, and warranty administrator rules can materially narrow public baselines.
unresolved_ambiguities.market_ready_resolution: Not blocking because final disposition requires tenant source records, agreement terms, payer/plan context, and source hierarchy; conflicts trigger orchestrator return.

unresolved_ambiguities.ambiguity: Home services and healthcare-linked service agreements may use overlapping terms such as coverage, entitlement, authorization, and service plan.
unresolved_ambiguities.market_ready_resolution: Not blocking because scenario sas-12 requires terminology normalization, assumption recording, and escalation when definitions are missing.
